Description: Completes a notebook experiment with various options to enhance the user experience in an NGS lab
Tag Help:
Plugins
Overview
The complete workflow button will give the user one button to manage the completion of a notebook. This button will provide consistency across ELN templates throughout a process while allowing flexibility for admins to dictate whether all entries need to be completed prior to notebook completion or if e-signatures are required.
Description
The SHOW COMPLETE WORKFLOW BUTTON tag must be present in the Notebook Options for this button to appear.
All option values are not required. If no option values are given, the following defaults will apply:
E-Signatures are required
All entries must be validated prior to completing the workflow
Allows anyone with 'write' access (Author or Owner) to complete the workflow
Values/Modifiers
Modifiers give the administrator a means to adjust the Complete Workflow button to fit the needs of the lab without requiring consulting work. The following option values are available:
ESIGN NOT REQUIRED
By default, esign is required by any user attempting to complete a workflow. If present, users with proper permissions (Authors and Owners) are able to complete the workflow immediately without providing login credentials.
NO COMMENT REQUIRED FOR ESIGN
Prevents the comment box from appearing during e-sign allowing for only username/password to continue. Only applicable if 'ESIGN NOT REQUIRED' is not also present.
RESTRICT TO OWNER
The generic E-Sign button for the ELN restricts completion to only the Owner. By default, the Complete Workflow button allows anyone with Write access to be able to complete workflows. However, this can be overridden to function similarly to the E-Sign button and require the Owner to e-sign.
ALL ENTRY VALIDATIONS NOT REQUIRED
By default all entries will be required to be validated before, but with this tag you will be able to complete the workflow without validating all entries.
Experiment Entry Tags
WORKFLOW COMPLETED ON VALIDATION
If at least one experiment entry contains this tag, it is required to be validated prior to the workflow being completed. Any other entry in the experiment, other than those with the REQUIRES MANUAL VALIDATION, will not impact the completion of the workflow whether they are validated or not.
REQUIRES MANUAL VALIDATION
 Any experiment entry with this tag must be validated prior to the completion of the workflow.
E-Notebook Options
The following is a list of E-Notebook option keys.
Notebook Key: Show Complete Workflow Button
If this key is present, the "Complete Workflow" button will show in any experiment built from this template.
Permutations
SHOW COMPLETE WORKFLOW BUTTON
Tag Values
The following is a list of tag values for the task options.
Tag Value: Esign Not Required
If present, the user completing the workflow will not be prompted to verify login credentials
Permutations
ESIGN NOT REQUIRED
Tag Value: Restrict to Owner
If present, only the owner can complete the workflow.
Permutations
RESTRICT TO OWNER
Tag Value: All Entry Validations Not Required
This tag will allow the "Complete Workflow" button shown to be used without validating all entries.
NOTE: This tag will be overridden by the WORKFLOW COMPLETED ON VALIDATION and REQUIRES MANUAL VALIDATION entry level tags. If those tags are present on any Experiment Entry, those corresponding entries will require validation prior to completing the workflow.
Permutations
ALL ENTRY VALIDATIONS NOT REQUIRED
ALL ENTRY VALIDATIONS NOT REQUIRED, ESIGN NOT REQUIRED
Tag Value: No Comment Required
E-Sign dialog does not prompt for or require a comment to be entered for an e-signature to be accepted. Only applies if 'ESIGN NOT REQUIRED' is not present. 
Permutations
NO COMMENT REQUIRED FOR ESIGN
Tag Value: Disable PDF Generation
Disables the PDF Generation on using the Complete Workflow button
Permutations
DISABLE PDF GENERATION
Notebook Key: Hide Complete Workflow Button
If this key is present, the "Complete Workflow" button will not show in any experiment built from this template.
Permutations
HIDE COMPLETE WORKFLOW BUTTON
E-Notebook Entry Options
The following is a list of E-Notebook Entry option keys.
Notebook Entry Key: Workflow Completed on Validation
Applicable Entry Types: Attachment,Form,Plugin,Table
When the "Complete Workflow" button is triggered, the entry containing this tag is required to be submitted in order for the workflow to be successfully completed. 
Any other entry that is not validated will be considered completed as long as this entry is completed unless containing the "REQUIRES MANUAL VALIDATION" tag
Permutations
WORKFLOW COMPLETED ON VALIDATION
Notebook Entry Key: Requires Manual Validation
Applicable Entry Types: Attachment,Form,Plugin,Table
If present, the associated Experiment Entry must be validated by the user (thereby running any associated initialization or validation plugins) prior to completing a workflow. 
This tag will override the Notebook Entry Option Value "ALL ENTRY VALIDATIONS NOT REQUIRED".
Permutations
REQUIRES MANUAL VALIDATION

Tag Name: Create Batch For Next Process Workflow
Description: This tag runs on submit of a task and gathers all samples and plate wells attached to current active task and creates a Batch record with a Batch Workflow of the next process workflow those samples are assigned to. It is given a unique Batch Id and it sets the Batch name to either the StorageUnitId of the Storage Unit those samples are stored or to the Plate Id if the task option value \"PlateWell\" is  associated with the task option.
Tag Help:
Plugins
CreateBatchOnSubmit [Task: Submit] (LATE + 2)
CREATE BATCH FOR NEXT PROCESS WORKFLOW task option runs on submit of a task and gathers all samples and plate wells attached to current active task and creates a Batch record with a Batch Workflow of the next process workflow those samples are assigned to. It is given a unique Batch Id and it sets the Batch name to either the StorageUnitId of the Storage Unit those samples are stored or to the Plate Id if the task option value "PlateWell" is associated with the task option.
"GROUP RECORDS BY FIELD: dataTypeName(fieldName)"
This is a task option value used with the CREATE BATCH FOR NEXT PROCESS WORKFLOW task option key. Defining this value indicates that all Sample and PlateWell records that are ready for further processing and that are attached to the current task should be batched in accordance to some specified set of attributes.
Attribute specification is performed by defining an ancestor related to the to-be-batched samples and a set of field names. The system will then group all samples sharing the same values in the indicated ancestor fields and create a batch from the grouped samples that will be set as ready for the next workflow in the process associated with batched samples.
Note: The next workflow in the process is used by default as a grouping attribute, so that samples moving onto workfow a will not be in the same batch as samples moving onto workflow b.
Defining an ancestor data type is done by replacing the dataTypeName value in this task option value with the proper data type name. Similarly, defining the fields that are to be used in sample grouping is done by replacing the fieldName value in the task option value with appropriate field names. A delimiter for field names is a colon (:). Additionally, if the samples themselves are to be used as records containing attribute values, use the keyword this in place of the data type name.
Upon batch creation, the batch name will be set to consist of the attribute values used in its creation.
EXAMPLES
Task Option Key	Task Option Value	Description
CREATE BATCH FOR NEXT PROCESS WORKFLOW	GROUP RECORDS BY FIELD: Project(ProjectId)	Create batches of samples where each batch will consist of samples or plate wells related to the same Project record.
CREATE BATCH FOR NEXT PROCESS WORKFLOW	GROUP RECORDS BY FIELD: this(ExemplarSampleType : Species)	Create batches of samples where each batch will consist of samples or plate wells that have identical sample types and that are of the same species.
"USE EXISTING BATCH IF AVAILABLE"
This is a task option value used with the CREATE BATCH FOR NEXT PROCESS WORKFLOW task option key and the GROUP RECORDS BY FIELD: dataTypeName(fieldName) task option value. This value indicates that when the samples and plate wells are grouped in accordance to specifications defined in the GROUP RECORDS BY FIELD: dataTypeName(fieldName) task option value, then instead of immediately creating a batch, they system should first attempt to update an existing batch that is defined with the same parameters.
Note: If an existing batch is found, it will only be updated if it does not have any parent records associated with it. A batch with parent records is regarded as one that has completed it's associated workflow or is in process of one.
EXAMPLE
Task Option Key	Task Option Value	Description
CREATE BATCH FOR NEXT PROCESS WORKFLOW	GROUP RECORDS BY FIELD: Project(ProjectId), USE EXISTING BATCH IF AVAILABLE	Create batches of samples where each batch will consist of samples or plate wells related to the same Project record. If a batch with same parameters already exists, and is not in or completed a workflow, it will be updated with these additional samples.
Task Option Keys
The following is a list of task option keys that can be used on workflow tasks.
Task Key: Create Batch For Next Process Workflow
Permutations
CREATE BATCH FOR NEXT PROCESS WORKFLOW
Tag Values
The following is a list of tag values for the task options.
Tag Value: Group Records By Field
Permutations
.*GROUP RECORDS BY FIELD:\s*([a-zA-Z0-9]+)\(\s*([a-zA-Z0-9,\s]+)\s*\).*
Tag Value: Use Existing Batch If Available
Permutations
.*USE\s+EXISTING\s+BATCH\s+IF\s+AVAILABLE.*
E-Notebook Options
The following is a list of E-Notebook option keys.
Notebook Key: Create Batch For Next Process Workflow For ELN
On E-Sign complete of a notebook, if the notebook has this tag, it will do the following:
Take the lowest hierarchical samples (aliquots/pools, etc.) of samples in the notebook.
If they are not failed, they will add them to a batch
Then the batch automatically queued using one of the assigned processes of one of the samples.
Permutations
CREATE BATCH FOR NEXT PROCESS WORKFLOW

Description: These are a collection of plugins which supports E-signature features.
Tag Help:
Plugins
LockEntryValidation [ENotebook Entry: Validation] (MIDDLE)
LockNotebook [ENotebook: Main Toolbar] (MIDDLE)
UnlockEntry [ENotebook Entry: Toolbar] (MIDDLE)
UnlockNotebook [ENotebook: Main Toolbar] (MIDDLE)
ViewNotebookEntryESignature [ENotebook Entry: Toolbar] (MIDDLE)
ViewNotebookESignature [ENotebook: Main Toolbar] (MIDDLE)
LockEntryToolbar [ENotebook Entry: Toolbar] (MIDDLE)
E-Signature for Electronic Notebook
Sapio ELN allows users to e-sign notebooks and individual entries in notebooks and work them throughout an approval process. The plugins are tag driven so the button would only appear on buttons where e-signature makes sense. It is possible to turn on and off the features individually. For example, some notebooks may not allow ELN admins to unlock, some notebooks always requires approval, some notebooks may require approvals for entries but not the notebook. This documentation describes how to use all of these options.
Any user who have read access to a notebook may put their signature on a notebook with signing enabled for the user role. However, the effect of the signature depends on the user role in the notebook. Owner will first sign with the notebook unlocked, then other users would sign when the notebook is locked. Once the notebooks are locked, authors may no longer edit. Depending on configurations, all other authors may be required to sign at this point. Afterwards, approvers, reviewers, and witnesses may sign as long as their signatures are required and the notebook is in the state awaiting for approval.
The configurations may be defined by either Exemplar ELN Configurations, or notebook and entry options on templates. The template options takes priority over settings defined in Exemplar ELN Configurations.
Notebooks may require some or all entries to be completed before anyone can continue with E-Sign process. This is customizable through additional experiment and entry keys. See key descriptions for details.
The Approval Process
(Owner Starts E-Sign Process) -> (Optional: all other authors signs) -> (Optional: One Witness) -> (Optional: One Reviewer) -> (Optional: One Approver)
These are steps a notebook/entry would go through for a typical approval process:
The notebook/entry has e-sign signature enabled.
The author finish all changes to the notebook finishes assigning user roles. Then either one author or the owner signs off the notebook. At this point, all user roles are locked in. This means no one can change the author/approver/reviewer/witness list. During the sign-off, the author enters an approval due date. The due date is optional and will appear in log and notifications if specified.
Everyone who is not the author or owner whose signatures are required by the tag option now signs off. The order is Witness->Reviewer->Approver. Once a user of that role signs off, E-sign will progress to the next role and no other users in the same role may change the verdict.
During signing, any user may enter a verdict. Even if another user in the same role had previously accepted the notebook or entry.
If the verdict is Accepted
If this is the last signature required, then the status will be changed to Completed - Approved.
If it still needs more signatures, then the status will still be Locked - Awaiting Approval. But a notification will still be sent to the authors and owners.
If the verdict is Rejected, the notebook is marked as Rejected - Final, and cannot be edited further.
If the verdict is Changes Required, the notebook is marked as Unlocked - Changes Required. Go back to step 2.
A notification will be sent to every author and owner of the notebook.
Owners and Authors
Only owner may start E-sign process. Other authors may not sign until owner signs first.
If owner and author are both specified in options,the owner must initiate the E-sign process. Afterwards, all authors must complete e-sign before approver/reviewer/witnesses are notified for their E-signature. If some author want to make changes the author can unlock the notebook.
If owner is specified in options, but author is not, then only the owner may E-sign.Other authors do not participate in this E-sign process.
E-Sign Notebooks Without Approval
Notebooks which are not eligible for the approval process may be signed by authors to lock the notebook and set the notebook status to Completed. After the notebook is locked, authors are no longer allowed to edit the notebook.
This is enabled by the tag option "Owner", which specifies only owner signatures are required, or not having a tag option at all and also leave E-Signature fields blank in exemplar configurations.
ELN Admin Unlock
An ELN admin may always unlock any notebook or any notebook entry as long as it makes sense. They will first acquire access to the notebook, then the unlock button will appear for them to unlock the notebook or notebook entry.
An ELN admin may unlock the notebook if and only if the status of the notebook is Locked(Waiting for Approval/Rejected Final) or Completed(Completed or Completed with Approval).
An ELN admin may unlock an entry in notebook if and only if all of the followings are true:
The notebook is currently unlocked. If the notebook is locked, the admin needs to first unlock the notebook before unlocking any entries in the notebook.
The status of the entry is Locked(Waiting for Approval/Rejected Final) or Completed(Completed or Completed with Approval).
Once the ELN admin performs any of these actions, all authors of the notebook will be notified about the unlock action via both broadcast message and email. This notification cannot be suppressed by tag options.
The admin unlock is a special e-sign action which also requires the admin to re-authenticate.
Only the ELN Admin, owner of the experiment, and the original creator of the entry may unlock the entry while the entry is locked during E-sign process.
Authentication Form
The authentication form will be displayed for each E-sign action, including locking and unlocking. The form may provide additional fields to fill. Author E-sign requires authors to fill in a due date, Approver E-Sign requires approvers to fill in a verdict.
All e-signatures requires the username of the e-authentication form to match the username of the user logged in. It also requires the password to match the password of the current user. The comment is optional and will be shown in the view e-sign history.
E-Notebook Options
The following is a list of E-Notebook option keys.

When this tag is on the notebook, a toolbar button will be shown on the notebook toolbar. 
The toolbar button will not be shown if the user is not an author and no signature is required for the user role specified in the tag value. See the tag value for details.
Permutations
SHOW ELN MAIN TOOLBAR ESIGN BUTTON
Tag Values
The following is a list of tag values for the task options.

The notebook is marked as completed only if there is one user in each role who have signed it before after the last unlock E-signature.
Author must be present for the tag to function.
If owner is specified, only owner may start the signing process. If author and owner are both specified, and another author exists in notebook, then it requires both the owner and another author to sign.
Permutations
Author
Owner
Owner:Author
Owner:Author:Approver
Author:Approver
Author:Approver:Reviewer:Witness

When this is on the notebook, a view history button will appear on the notebook's main toolbar to view notebook-level signatures.

Additional, if a data type (or data types) is (are) added to the tag's value (comma separated), only those experiment locations will be used during the looking. Using the first one found based on the list.
Example:
PROCESS QUEUE USE ANCESTOR EXPERIMENT LOCATION ::: Project,VeloxLocation
Permutations
PROCESS QUEUE USE ANCESTOR EXPERIMENT LOCATION
E-Notebook Entry Options
The following is a list of E-Notebook Entry option keys.
Notebook Entry Key: Disable Entry Locking
Applicable Entry Types: Form,Table
Prevent a entry that is populated on start of a workflow from the process queue from being submitted.
Permutations
DISABLE AUTOMATIC ENTRY LOCKING BY QUEUE

Description: This tag will stop Process Tracking from running when a notebook completes.
Tag Help:
Plugins
ProcessTrackingNotebookStatusDelegate [ENotebook: Status Change] (LATE)
This tag will be used in a check to determine if process tracking should run. If the tag is present, when a notebook experiment is completed, process tracking code will not run.
The intent of this tag was to allow an overriding of the default process tracking for a specific notebook.
E-Notebook Options
The following is a list of E-Notebook option keys.
Notebook Key: Process Tracking Ignore
This tag allows for the default process tracking, that runs at the completion of a workflow, to be overwritten.
Permutations
PROCESS TRACKING IGNORE

Tag Name: Redirect User on Experiment Status Change
Description: Redirects the user elsewhere when the experiment's status changes
Tag Help:
Plugins
Sends the user to another location when the status is changed for an experiment. Multiple tags may be added to cover multiple scenarios.
Note: This tag runs on certain experiment buttons (it's not possible to run on status change invocation).
Usage: SEND <ELN ROLE> TO <LOCATION> ON <STATUS>
e.g. SEND USER TO HOMEPAGE ON COMPLETION
Valid ELN ROLEs:
- USER (Ignores role and applies to all users)
- OWNER
- AUTHOR
- WITNESS
- REVIEWER
- APPROVER
Valid LOCATIONs:
- HOMEPAGE
- WORKQUEUE
- PROCESSQUEUE
- NEXTNOTEBOOK
Valid STATUSes:
- COMPLETION (If status is "Completed" or "Completed - Approved")
- CANCEL (If status is "Locked - Rejected By Approver")
- LOCK (If status is "Locked - Awaiting Approval", or any of the COMPLETION or CANCEL statuses)
E-Notebook Options
The following is a list of E-Notebook option keys.
Notebook Key: SEND ROLE TO PLACE ON STATUS
Permutations
SEND USER TO HOMEPAGE ON COMPLETION
SEND OWNER TO PROCESSQUEUE ON CANCEL
SEND AUTHOR TO HOMEPAGE ON LOCK
SEND WITNESS TO WORKQUEUE ON COMPLETION
SEND REVIEWER TO HOMEPAGE ON CANCEL
SEND APPROVER TO WORKQUEUE ON LOCK

Description: Restricts that a notebook created under a template can only go under a specific project and/or study.
Tag Help:
Plugins
RestrictTemplateToProject [ENotebook: Status Change] (FIRST)
Restricts the template so that any experiments created with it can only be set under the project/study that matches the ProjectId/StudyId given in the tag value. 
E-Notebook Options
The following is a list of E-Notebook option keys.
Notebook Key: Restrict This Template To Project
RESTRICT_THIS_TEMPLATE_TO_PROJECT:::ProjectId
Disallows the use of this template from being added under any other project, or from studies that are not descendants of the given project.
Permutations
RESTRICT THIS TEMPLATE TO PROJECT
Tag Values
The following is a list of tag values for the task options.
Tag Value: ProjectId
Permutations
REPLACE THIS VALUE WITH PROJECT ID
Notebook Key: Restrict This Template To Study
RESTRICT_THIS_TEMPLATE_TO_STUDY:::StudyId
Prevents this template from being set under any study other than the one matching the given StudyId.
Permutations
RESTRICT THIS TEMPLATE TO STUDY
Tag Values
The following is a list of tag values for the task options.
Tag Value: StudyId
Permutations
REPLACE THIS VALUE WITH STUDY ID
